rulings.Case Title: IN RE: QUALITY LOAN SERVICE CORP., TRUSTEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Distribute The Surplus Funds On Deposit With The Court From The Trustees Sale Of 117 Marlin Court #49, San Francisco, Ca 94124 To The Heir Of The Deceased Former Owner
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 17, 2025, line 1. Respondent's Motion to Distribute the Surplus Funds on Deposit with the Court From the Trustee Sale is DENIED without prejudice. Claimant failed to sufficiently address why the initial deadline to file a claim with the Trustee was missed. In the moving papers, but not in any of the declarations, Claimant contends there is "no evidence" that Claimant received notice from the Trustee. But the proof of service attached to the Podmernik declaration as Exhibit C filed on May 25, 2023, shows that Ruby Lee Gunn was served with Notice at her address in Miami, Florida on August 31, 2022. The proof of service attached as Exh. E to the same declaration shows Ruby Lee Gunn was also served with Notice of Deposit Surplus Funds on February 15, 2023 at the same address. Claimant has also not provided sufficient evidence supporting her claim. Claimant must submit documentation demonstrating that she is the sole heir of Janice Gunn's estate and that Janice Gunn owned the property at the time of the foreclosure. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AVI EDRI VS. TAMAR RACHEL KASSOFF-SOFIOV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedures 664.6 And Supporting Declarations
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 17, 2025, line 2. Defendant's Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant to CCP 664.6 is OFF CALENDAR for failure to comply with LRSF 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of the moving papers. rulings.Case Title: THAI MING CHIU VS. CRYSTAL LEI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration And Relief Under Ccp Sections 1008 And 473
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 17, 2025, line 3. Motion for Reconsideration and Relief Under CCP Sections 1008 and 473 is GRANTED. The Motion for Summary Judgment hearing was continued by an order that took Defendant's counsel by surprise, which justifies relief under CCP Section 473. As such, the Court orders that the Motion for Summary Judgment currently set for hearing on October 8, 2025 be advanced to August 19, 2025 at 9:30 am in Department 501. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 25 ESSEX L.P. VS. DARRYL BROWN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 17, 2025, line 6. Plaintiff's Motion for Judgment Pursuant to Stipulation is OFF CALENDAR. Defendant is represented limited scope. All papers in the case must be served both on the defendant's limited scope attorney and on the defendant. (CRC 3.36.) The proofs of service indicate that the moving papers were served on the limited scope attorney only. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
